messages.content: mysql connector net 64bit

The MySQL Connector/Net is a fully-managed ADO.NET driver for MySQL that allows developers to access and interact with MySQL databases from .NET applications. 

To download the 64-bit version of the MySQL Connector/Net, you can visit the official MySQL website or use the following link:

https://dev.mysql.com/downloads/connector/net/

On the downloads page, select the appropriate version of the Connector/Net for your specific .NET framework version and operating system. Make sure to choose the 64-bit version if that's what you need.

After downloading the installer, run it and follow the installation prompts to install the MySQL Connector/Net on your machine.
but there is no 64-bit

I apologize for the confusion. It appears that there is currently no separate 64-bit version of the MySQL Connector/Net. Instead, the Connector/Net is designed to be platform-agnostic and can be used with both 32-bit and 64-bit applications.

When you download and install the Connector/Net, it will work with both 32-bit and 64-bit applications, depending on the target platform of your .NET application.

You can download the latest version of the MySQL Connector/Net from the following link:

https://dev.mysql.com/downloads/connector/net/

GET https://unpkg.com/xlsx-js-style@1.2.0/dist/dist.min.js 404

I apologize for the inconvenience. It seems that the requested resource is not available at the provided URL. 

Please ensure that you have the correct version of xlsx-js-style library. You can try the following alternatives:

1. Use a different version: Update the version number in the URL to a valid version. You can check the available versions on the npm website (https://www.npmjs.com/package/xlsx-js-style).

2. Use a different CDN or hosting service: Instead of using the unpkg CDN, you can try using a different CDN or hosting the xlsx-js-style library on your own server.

3. Download and host the library locally: Download the xlsx-js-style library manually from a reliable source and host it on your own server. Then update the script tag in your HTML file to point to the locally hosted version.

Please note that availability and accessibility of external libraries can change over time and may vary based on the library version. It's always a good practice to check the official documentation or the library's website for the latest information and appropriate URLs to download or access the library.
